title: centaurea polymorpha
intro: Centaurea polymorpha, also known as the Mexican prickle poppy or golden tickseed, is a flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Centaurea polymorpha include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Centaurea polymorpha can be found in teas, tinctures, and salves, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
